items.title: Former Apple engineer raises $5M for a note-taking pendant that only records your voice
items.content: Taya is trying to address privacy concerns around note-taking wearables with its device that can be worn as a pendant and records only the user's voice.

items.title: US military contractor likely built iPhone hacking tools used by Russian spies in Ukraine

items.title: Apple now makes 1 in 4 iPhones in India: Report
items.content: Apple reportedly produced 55 million units in India last year, a shift accelerated as Apple faced growing uncertainty in China.

items.title: The billion-dollar infrastructure deals powering the AI boom
items.content: Here's everything we know about the biggest AI infrastructure projects, including major spending from Meta, Oracle, Microsoft, Google, and OpenAI.

items.title: The White House wants AI companies to cover rate hikes. Most have already said they would.
items.content: Many hyperscalers have already made public commitments to cover electricity cost increases.

items.title: The public opposition to AI infrastructure is heating up
items.content: Public backlash over the data center boom is leading to a variety of draconian policies — including bans on new construction.

items.title: Trump’s Actions in Iran and Venezuela Show Limits of U.S. Sanctions
items.content: America’s vast economic powers are able to wear down an adversary’s economy but are insufficient to topple leaders on their own.

items.title: Consumer prices stayed subdued in February.
At Least 3 Ships Are Struck In and Around the Strait of Hormuz
items.content: Three vessels were hit on Wednesday, according to a British maritime agency. The Strait of Hormuz is all but closed as war in the Middle East expands.

items.title: Japan and Germany Say They Will Release Oil
items.content: Officials in Tokyo and Berlin said they planned to release oil from their strategic reserves to address disruptions of supplies from the Middle East.
